checked_queries: width height duration milliseconds multimodal Phase 2
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Dedicated delivered-output Phase 2 pricing is absent for image and video, and the generic Phase 2 calculator rejects media addresses; only audio duration-based Phase 2 pricing is present.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-async-fees/src/lib.rs implements audio duration extraction and audio Phase 2 fee calculation only.
crates/ethereum/payload/src/ritual_async.rs branches to audio fee calculation but falls back to generic Phase 2 for image/video, which rejects unsupported addresses.
ritual-revm-internal image/video precompiles calculate gas by input/output bytes, not executor fees from delivered dimensions/duration.

notes: Audio has a partial implementation, but the invariant requires image, audio, and video delivered-output attributes plus generic rejection guidance.
rationale: The merge adds extraction utilities for image/audio/video delivery results, dedicated Phase 2 fee calculators, and payload-builder branches that extract delivered output attributes before calculating executor fees. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Phase 2 fee calculation for image, audio, and video precompiles must use delivery-result attributes: image width and height, audio duration seconds, and video width, height, and duration milliseconds. Generic block-based Phase 2 pricing must reject these precompiles and direct callers to the dedicated multimodal calculators.
suggested_falsification_tests: Provide Phase 2 delivery results for image, audio, and video with known dimensions/duration and assert payload fee calculation uses the dedicated calculators; call the generic calculate_async_fees_phase2 with these precompile addresses and assert it returns the guidance error.
summary: Multimodal Phase 2 fee calculation is incomplete.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Provide Phase 2 delivery results for image, audio, and video with known dimensions/duration and assert payload fee calculation uses the dedicated calculators; call the generic calculate_async_fees_phase2 with these precompile addresses and assert it returns the guidance error.
title: Multimodal Phase 2 fees use delivered output attributes
